sourceType: Web scan (Polymarket docs, analytics, news outlets)
reliability: High (official Polymarket docs, Phemex, Pine Analytics)
scanType: Focused scan: Polymarket fee expansion (goes live March 30 - TOMORROW)
summary: TOMORROW (March 30): Polymarket expands taker fees to 8 new categories (Politics, Finance, Economics, Culture, Weather, Tech, Mentions, Other/General). Total fee-bearing categories: 10. GEOPOLITICS AND WORLD EVENTS REMAIN FREE.
Revenue projection: $800K-$1M daily from fees. Based on $9.55B trailing 30-day volume.

Maker Rebates Program: makers (resting orders) pay ZERO fees and receive daily USDC rebates funded by taker fees. Rebate rates: Finance 50%, all others 25%. This incentivizes market-making and deepens orderbooks.
Early evidence from Crypto and Sports fee rollouts: Sports showed NO volume decline. Crypto saw only slight pullback within normal variance. Fee introduction is not killing volume.
Crypto category fee increased from 1.56% to 1.80% (15% increase on existing traders).

Geopolitics/world events staying fee-free is strategically important: this is the highest-volume category ($464.5M+) and keeping it free maintains Polymarket's dominance in the war/conflict markets that are driving current demand.

leverImplications.oracleImpact: Fees should not materially affect LEVER's oracle quality. CLOB midpoint pricing is determined by resting orders (makers), and makers are INCENTIVIZED by rebates. More maker liquidity = tighter spreads = better PI quality for LEVER.
leverImplications.volumeRisk: If fees cause volume decline in specific categories, those markets may become less liquid for LEVER to reference. Monitor post-March-30 volume by category.
leverImplications.competitiveOpportunity: Polymarket ending zero-fee era may push some price-sensitive traders to alternatives. XMarket (fee-free during beta) and Limitless could benefit. But the maker rebate program should retain serious liquidity providers.
leverImplications.geopoliticsFreeIsGood: Geopolitics staying fee-free means the highest-volume category (Iran war, Ukraine, elections) remains maximally liquid. This is LEVER's primary market category.

workstreamTags.BUILD: POSITIVE for LEVER's oracle: Polymarket's maker rebate program incentivizes resting orders (the source of CLOB midpoint data). More maker liquidity = tighter spreads = better PI quality. Monitor post-March-30 volume to verify no category-specific decline. Geopolitics staying free is ideal for LEVER's primary market focus.
workstreamTags.CEO: Polymarket generating $800K-$1M/day from fees validates prediction market monetization. At $365M/year revenue, that supports the $20B valuation at ~55x revenue (growth premium). LEVER's 50/30/20 fee split generates revenue from day one; cite Polymarket's fee model as proof of monetization thesis.
workstreamTags.ADVISOR: Polymarket ending free trading is a market maturation signal. Fee introduction without volume decline (early evidence) means the market has pricing power. This is positive for the entire sector including LEVER: if users accept prediction market fees, they will accept leverage fees.
workstreamTags.IMPROVE: Polymarket's maker rebate UX (daily USDC payouts, proportional to executed maker volume) is a reference for how LEVER should display LP rewards. Simple, transparent, daily.
